blocks.content: June. Millhaven Baptist Church. The same building Kit Jenkins had sat in on forty-two consecutive Christmas Eves and fallen asleep during every single one.
He stood at the front of the sanctuary in a charcoal suit his mother had picked out and Eli had pressed that morning, and he watched the doors at the back of the room.
Daisy May Jones, in ivory, with her mother's pearls at her collarbone and her hair soft and a little loose.
She reached him. Her father placed her hand in his. Then it was just the two of them.
She tightened her hand around his.
